title: Gymnanthemum reticulatum
taxonomy: Gymnanthemum reticulatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This species is further classified within the genus Gymnanthemum, a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family Asteraceae is one of the largest and most widespread plant families in the world, containing over 23,000 known species. Gymnanthemum reticulatum is a specific example of a flowering plant within this large and diverse group. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the family Asteraceae.
uses: Gymnanthemum reticulatum, a species of flowering plant, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its leaves and stems have been utilized to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which have shown potential in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often employed to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. However, further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of Gymnanthemum reticulatum for medicinal use.

preparations: Gymnanthemum reticulatum is traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions. side_effects: Gymnanthemum reticulatum, also known as the stink plant or false lemongrass, contains compounds that may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to its essential oils can lead to headaches and dizziness. The plant's strong scent can also trigger respiratory issues in people with sensitivities. Additionally, ingesting any part of the plant can result in gastrointestinal problems and vomiting. It is essential to exercise caution when handling or using Gymnanthemum reticulatum, especially for those with pre-existing health conditions.
